# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# frozen_string_literal: true

Rails.application.config.action_dispatch.cookies_serializer = :json

module SafeSessionStore
  class CookieMarshalStore
    SESSION_COOKIE_KEY = :session

    def self.load(cookies)
      raw = cookies[SESSION_COOKIE_KEY]
      return {} if raw.nil? || raw.empty?

      payload = JSON.parse(Base64.decode64(raw))
      payload.is_a?(Hash) ? payload : {}
    rescue JSON::ParserError, ArgumentError
      {}
    end

    def self.dump(cookies, session_data, expires: nil, httponly: true, secure: Rails.env.production?, same_site: :lax)
      serialized = Base64.strict_encode64(JSON.generate(session_data.to_h))
      cookies[SESSION_COOKIE_KEY] = {
        value: serialized,
        expires: expires,
        httponly: httponly,
        secure: secure,
        same_site: same_site
      }
    end

    def self.clear(cookies)
      cookies.delete(SESSION_COOKIE_KEY)
    end
  end
end
